ogTitle: Come By Chance vs Comerong Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Come By Chance and Comerong Island.
heading: Come By Chance vs Comerong Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Come By Chance, New South Wales and Comerong Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Come By Chance has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Comerong Island (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Come By Chance or Comerong Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Come By Chance has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Comerong Island scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Come By Chance scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Comerong Island scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Come By Chance or Comerong Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Come By Chance has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
